claim: Spontaneous association: alarm clock/sharp ring
quote: the first thing that pops into my head is, like, some alarm clock, that sharp ringing sound

claim: Second spontaneous association: school gym class/gym, tone rather neutral-ironic-annoyed
quote: I also remember gym class in school, I can literally picture that gym

claim: Respondent explicitly labels the stated attitude as fine, without tension
quote: a fine brand, nothing weird, the name's just a bit funny

claim: The name conveys the idea of energy/perking up
quote: It conveys, basically, that it's about energy, about perking up and running off back to work

claim: The name hides the quality of the coffee and the barista's work, causing the substance to get lost
quote: doesn't convey at all is that the coffee there is actually good, I mean the baristas are solid

claim: The name is hard to recall, and in conversation gets replaced by a description of the place
quote: the name just doesn't come to mind on its own

claim: Proposed name options: Jolt and Pulse
quote: something like "Jolt" or "Pulse," short, punchy

claim: Jolt is liked because it keeps the idea of energy without the schoolteacher-ish alarm-clock undertone
quote: I like "Jolt" because it keeps that idea of energy

claim: Explicitly rejected option: a foreign phrase like Energy Cup, sounds cheap
quote: not go with something with a foreign word like "Energy Cup"

claim: Reference outside the category — burger chain Crafthouse, values minimalism and black-and-white signage
quote: I like how it's done at this one burger chain, called "Crafthouse," they've got minimalism in the signage, black-and-white, stylish

claim: Reference inside the category — Starbucks as the first brand that comes to mind for coffee in general
quote: Starbucks obviously, just because that's the first thing everyone thinks of for a coffee brand at all

claim: Logo — yellow lightning bolt on a mug, acid color, loud
quote: a yellow lightning bolt on a mug, pretty loud, I'd say straight-up acid yellow

claim: Physical signage in poor condition — faded, letters peeling
quote: The sign at the actual location has already faded a bit, the letters are peeling on the left

claim: Website is outdated, social media is run irregularly, seemingly by one person
quote: Honestly their website's so-so, I went there once to check the menu, and it was just some old version sitting there

claim: Name-recognition problem despite preserved recognition of visual elements
quote: if I tell someone "Perky," half of them ask what that even is

claim: Stated target audience — office workers grabbing coffee to go in the morning
quote: it's for office people who are rushing in the morning, strictly grab-and-go, quick and off to work

claim: Gap: the actual audience includes students in the evening, which doesn't match the place's style
quote: in reality students also go there in the evening to sit around, and that's strange, because the sign and the whole style are absolutely not about sitting around

claim: Process recommendation: ask regular customers first, don't change things abruptly
quote: asking regular customers, not just go and swap out the sign overnight

claim: Risk of an abrupt change — people won't recognize it's the same place and will walk past out of habit
quote: some people just won't realize it's the same place, and they'll walk right past out of habit

claim: UNSTABLE: the respondent himself cannot pin down the brand's essence — a checkbox assembly line or a place that genuinely loves coffee
quote: I don't even know myself, is it a checkbox assembly line or a place that genuinely loves coffee, depends on the day, I guess

claim: Gap between the stated grab-and-go-for-office positioning and the actual mixed use by evening students
quote: it's like they haven't figured out for themselves who this place is actually for

claim: HIGH rebranding risk — three years of built-up recognition through color/lightning bolt, a mismatch between name and visuals would confuse customers
quote: if you suddenly change the name but keep the colors and the lightning bolt, everyone will just get confused, that's part of the recognition too

claim: Fault line: the source of impressions differs — personal experience with Perky and Coffee Cult vs. a friend's account of BlackCat
quote: my friend told me about that one, that it's all really stylish there, black cups, that kind of minimalism

claim: Key insight: the brand is perceived as grab-and-go energy for office workers, but the substance gets lost and recognition rests on the visuals rather than the name, so any renaming should be gradual and preserve the visual code
quote: the name just doesn't come to mind on its own
